title: Not compatible with frozen strings
author: morgoth
body:
Hi @weppos 

I would like to setup my app to have frozen strings by default.
Example description: https://www.mikeperham.com/2018/02/28/ruby-optimization-with-one-magic-comment/

Using tabs_on_rails is not possible with globally enabled frozen string. This can be seen by setting `RUBYOPT="--enable=frozen-string-literal"` and running Rails app.

When rendering tabs, the error is thrown: `ActionView::Template::Error (can't modify frozen String: "")`

title: Deprecation warnings in Rails 6
author: olimart
body:
Hi,

This
https://github.com/weppos/tabs_on_rails/blob/master/lib/tabs_on_rails/railtie.rb#L14
is causing the following warning in Rails 6 app.

`DEPRECATION WARNING: Initialization autoloaded the constants ActionText::ContentHelper and ActionText::TagHelper.`

Should use `:prepend` instead of `:include`.
Here is an example of a potential fix
https://github.com/mileszs/wicked_pdf/pull/869/files#diff-ea9340aeb7bd0bcc5782373373980c56R10-R15

I used the following snippet to track it down
https://github.com/rails/rails/issues/36546#issuecomment-558773961

Let me know if you need anything else. Thanks

title: Update Gem to support Rails 5.1
author: ghost
body:
Heya,

would you mind to update your gem to support Rails 5.1?

Currently you implemented its functionality by using `before_filter`. Those are deprecated are going to be removed with rails 5.1. They need to be replaced by `before_action`.

```
DEPRECATION WARNING: before_filter is deprecated and will be removed in Rails 5.1. Use before_action instead. (called from set_tab at /home/user/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.3.0/bundler/gems/tabs_on_rails-01ba07eca6fb/lib/tabs_on_rails/action_controller.rb:106)
```

title: No tabs
author: ojmurphy
body:
Hello, 

I placed the tabs_tag do |tab| command in my view, but instead of rendering any tabs, the view displays a bullet point list from my proposed tabs. Specifically: 
- User
- Group
- Settings

title: Add a class and id to the <ul>
author: turizoft
body:
For boostrap for example we need the ul to has the class 'nav nav-pills' for example in order to get the pills style:

`<ul class="nav nav-pills">`

It would be nice to have support for something like:

`<%= tabs_tag class: 'nav nav-pills', id: 'my-tab' do |tab| %>`

title: Controller#set_tab instance method implemented but not documented
author: jcamenisch
body:
I just spent a good bit of time writing ten different calls to `set_tab :x, if: ->{ condition }` in a controller, to set the current tab based on given params. I got it working perfectly, but hated the whole approach. It was begging to have set_tab called from a before_action filter instead of at the class level.

It finally dawned on me that that might be possible, even though the documentation says nothing about the capability. I checked the source, and there it is!

May I suggest mentioning this somewhere in the documentation? Would you consider a pull request if I worked on writing something up?

No promises, but I might be able to take some time to do that—if there's a chance it would be merged.

Thanks!

title: Can the current tab stay clickable?
author: 29thfloor
body:
Is there a way to make the current tab stay clickable? 

I'm using this for an app that has a top nav bar where certain tabs need to be highlighted when you are in a section that is below that top level section. So technically you're not on that page and should be able to click the tab to get back to it. But when I set the current tab it becomes unclickable.

title: wrong number of arguments (3 for 2)
author: alexmobo
body:
Here is my problem.

I've only one controller to navigate throught my primary tab menu...

> class StaticPagesController < ApplicationController
>  def home
>    set_tab :home
>  end
> ...
>  def facilities
>    set_tab :facilities
>    @facilities = Facility.all
> 
>    set_tab :second, :navigation, :only => %w(second)
>    set_tab :third, :navigation, :only => %w(third)
>    set_tab :first, :navigation, :only => %w(first)
>  end
> 
>  def first; end
>  def second; end
>  def third; end
> ...
>  def contact
>    set_tab :contact
>  end
> end

and I want to create a sub-menu inside facilities static page.

When I try to run this code, rails throws me the error:

> wrong number of arguments (3 for 2)

on line:

> set_tab :second, :navigation, :only => %w(second)

It seems that isn't finding the method:

> set_tab(*args)

Thanks in advanced,
Alex

title: Parameterize class of current tab
author: ruurd
body: Please make the CSS class name for the active tab  parameterizable. Currently the current tab is adorned with class="current" however for example bootstrap does the same with class="active".

title: Rails 3.2 and ActiveSupport::Concern deprecation warning
author: indrekj
body:
```
DEPRECATION WARNING: The InstanceMethods module inside ActiveSupport::Concern will be no longer included automatically. Please define instance methods directly in ActionController::Base instead.
```

I think it comes from lib/tabs_on_rails/action_controller.rb

body: I've implemented it with a tab builder, but that would be even cooler to set it as an option to a default builder.

title: Uninitialized constant (...builder => MenuTabBuilder...)
author: fabiochi
body:
Hi to all,
I got a uninitialized constant ActionView::CompiledTemplates::MenuTabBuilder
Extracted source (around line #15):

14:     <!-- prova tabs_on_rails -->
15:     <% tabs_tag (:builder => MenuTabBuilder, :open_tabs => { :id => "navigation" }) do |tab| %>
16:       <%= tab.home      I18n.t('.home'), pick_path %>
17:       <%= tab.buildings I18n.t('.buildings'), buildings_path %>
18:       <%= tab.customers I18n.t('.customers'), customers_path %>

Here the code /lib/menu_tab_builder.rb 

class MenuTabBuilder < TabsOnRails::Tabs::Builder
    def tab_for(tab, name, options, item_options = {})
      item_options[:class] = (current_tab?(tab) ? 'menu-sel' : '')
      @context.content_tag(:li, item_options) do
        @context.link_to(name, options)
      end
    end
end

Where's the error?

Fabio

title: undefined method `tabs_tag' with 1.3.2 and 2.0.0.pre
author: incubus
body:
Hi!

 I got a "undefined method `tabs_tag'" error with 1.3.2 and 2.0.0.pre versions. With 1.3.1 everything  is ok.

$ rails -v

Rails 3.0.3

$ ruby -v

ruby 1.9.2p0 (2010-08-18 revision 29036) [amd64-freebsd9]

$ cat Gemfile

source 'http://rubygems.org'

gem 'rails', '3.0.3'

gem 'active_scaffold'
gem 'ancestry'
gem 'devise'
gem 'haml'
gem 'pg'
gem 'tabs_on_rails'
